entries.content: Historical business cycle index compiled by Leonard P. Ayres (1939) for the Cleveland Trust Company. Monthly data (~12 observations per year) covering 1831-1939. The index is a composite indicator of economic activity with no direct modern equivalent—it predates NBER business cycle dates and modern GDP-based measures. Used to illustrate pre-1940 business cycle patterns.

Ayres, Leonard P. Turning Points in Business Cycles. Cleveland Trust Company, 1939. The Cleveland Trust Company business cycle index was constructed from a variety of 19th- and early 20th-century economic indicators. No extension planned—historical index only.

Figs 2.4A, 2.4B, and 2.4C display the Ayres business cycle index in three panels or sub-periods. The monthly frequency (~12 obs/year) allows identification of cycle turning points and amplitude. The series ends in 1939; there is no modern equivalent to extend the series. Used to demonstrate cyclical volatility in the pre-Great Depression era.

citations.full: Ayres, Leonard P. Turning Points in Business Cycles. Cleveland, OH: Cleveland Trust Company, 1939.
Cleveland Trust Company. Business Cycle Index. Historical economic indicators compiled by Leonard P. Ayres. 1831-1939.

methodology_summary: S004 is the Ayres (1939) business cycle index from the Cleveland Trust Company, monthly data 1831-1939. A historical composite indicator with no modern equivalent. Approximately 12 observations per year. Used for Figs 2.4A, 2.4B, 2.4C to illustrate pre-World War II business cycle patterns. No extension planned—the methodology and underlying indicators are not replicated in contemporary statistical agencies.
known_issues: Monthly frequency causes repeated year values in annual-format validators; inherent to source, not an error
